📋 Today's Flash Report Tuesday, June 17, 2025 · 8:00 AM

Auto-generated · Delivered to owner inbox
Deficiency Conversion Rate 41% ACTION REQUIRED — Call top 3 open quotes today
Wrench Time Utilization 72% On Track
Report Delivery Latency 4.8 hrs WATCH — Review Tech B, D, F queues
Cross-Service Penetration 2.1 / client ACTION REQUIRED — 6 single-service accounts need review

Weekly Gap Review — Owner Action Items
📞

Call Top 3 Open Quotes

Fairless Hills Retirement ($18.4K), Shore Memorial ($22.8K), and Bucks County Schools ($14.2K) have not accepted deficiency repair quotes past 5 days.

🔁

Cross-Sell Account Review

6 GTO-only clients have no Chemical supply agreement. Termac attachment rate target is 60%. Each conversion = ~$220/mo RMR.

⏱️

Report Delivery Audit

Techs B, D, and F averaging 6.2hr report delivery. Review if reports are being generated manually. Automation should bring this to <1hr.

🗺️

Driver D Route Review

Driver D trending at 61% wrench time this week vs 72% fleet average. Pull route log to identify excessive transit between stops.

✅

GTO Compliance — On Track

94% compliance cert delivery rate. 6 missed intervals flagged and assigned. No NFPA liability exposure this week.

💰

Revenue Ahead of Target

$1.24M MTD vs $1.10M target (+12.7%). UniPro and GTO leading. Filter Man slightly behind — 4 route completions pending.

Profitability Leak Detector — Operational Vital Signs

🔥 UniPro / Quality III — Fire Protection

Target vs Danger threshold
First-Time Fix Rate
Repairs completed in one trip · Every return trip = double transit + lost trust
🟡 Yellow: <83%  |  🔴 Red: <80%
84%
Deficiency-to-Quote Time
Hours from deficiency identified to quote issued · Fire hazards = liability exposure every hour
🟡 Yellow: 36–48hrs  |  🔴 Red: >48hrs
42 hrs
Deficiency Conversion Rate
Identified deficiencies → paid repair invoices
🟡 Yellow: 45–48%  |  🔴 Red: <45%
41%
ITM Renewal Rate
Recurring contracts renewed at term · Floor of the business
🟡 Yellow: 85–88%  |  🔴 Red: <85%
87%
Wrench Time Utilization
Billable on-site hours vs total hours worked
🟡 Yellow: 65–67%  |  🔴 Red: <65%
72%

🔩 AllPro Stainless — Fabrication

Shop floor vitals
Material Usage Variance
Actual vs estimated stainless steel usage · Every % over = margin gone
🟡 Yellow: 3–5%  |  🔴 Red: >5%
3.1%
Rework Rate
Jobs requiring correction due to fabrication errors · Most expensive cost in the shop
🟡 Yellow: 1.5–2%  |  🔴 Red: >2%
2.4%
Job Margin Variance (Est vs Actual)
Budget overrun across active fabrication jobs
🟡 Yellow: 3–5% over  |  🔴 Red: >5% over
-8.4%
Throughput Rate
Production vs 3-month rolling average · Drop signals shop bottleneck
🟡 Yellow: -7 to -10% vs avg  |  🔴 Red: >-10%
-3%
On-Time Delivery Rate
Projects completed by committed date
🟡 Yellow: 80–85%  |  🔴 Red: <80%
82%

🏢 Cross-Divisional — All Entities

Owner-level financial vitals
Cash-to-Cash Cycle
Days between paying labor/materials and receiving client payment · Kills working capital
🟡 Yellow: 37–45 days  |  🔴 Red: >45 days
38 days
Days Sales Outstanding (DSO)
Average days to collect on invoices
🟡 Yellow: 28–35 days  |  🔴 Red: >35 days
32 days
Cross-Service Penetration
Average services per client across the family
🟡 Yellow: 2.3–2.5  |  🔴 Red: <2.3
2.1

Blind Site Visit Stopped in, no appointment
Elevator Pitch
Tap each line as you deliver it. Advance when you're ready.

⚡ 30-Second Introduction

Hi, I'm Ted from Termac — we're a family of fire protection, kitchen, and facilities service companies covering the area.
We handle fire extinguisher inspections and kitchen suppression systems through UniPro, hood filter exchange through Filter Man, and grease trap service through G.T.O.
We also do custom stainless steel fabrication for commercial kitchens through ALLPro — tables, back walls, shelving — built and installed on-site.
And Termac handles dish machine service, commercial chemicals, and paper supplies — essentially everything a kitchen needs from one family of companies.
Most of our customers use us for two or three services. Can I take 5 minutes to show you exactly what we cover here?
Line 1 of 5
Which services is the customer interested in?
Check all that apply. Only selected services will generate surveys.
🔥
UniProFire extinguishers, suppression, monitoring
🌬️
Filter ManHood filter exchange & cleaning
🪣
G.T.O.Grease trap pumping & compliance
🔩
ALLProCustom stainless steel fabrication
🛡️
Quality IIIFire protection — Delaware territory
🧴
TermacDish machines, chemicals, paper products

📅
Schedule & Notes
Scheduling Note: A member of our scheduling team will contact the client directly to confirm the first available date and time. No date entry needed here.
✍️ Client Acceptance
Review the estimate with the client, then have them sign below to confirm they wish to proceed.
⚖️ Estimate Disclaimer — Client Initials Required
IMPORTANT NOTICE REGARDING SCOPE AND PRICING: The pricing set forth in this estimate constitutes an estimate based upon conditions observed during the initial compliance walkthrough conducted by Universal Fire Protection. This estimate is not a final or guaranteed price. The parties acknowledge and agree that the total amount due may be adjusted following the technician's on-site service visit to reflect actual findings, including but not limited to: (i) additional deficiencies identified during the service visit that were not observable during the walkthrough; (ii) additional parts, materials, or labor required to bring all systems into full compliance with applicable NFPA standards; (iii) corrections necessitated by conditions that were concealed, inaccessible, or not apparent at the time of the initial assessment. Universal Fire Protection shall notify the client of any material price adjustment prior to performing additional work. Client acknowledges that inspection fees, compliance fees, and the dispatch fee are non-refundable once service has been initiated.
By initialing, client confirms they have read, understand, and agree to the estimate disclaimer above. Initials are required before signing.
✍️
Client Signature
Step 1 — Generate signature from printed name
Uses the printed name entered above to create a signature
Step 2 — Review and confirm, or draw manually below

or click/drag here to draw manually

🌬️ Filter Man — Hood Filter Survey
Take a photo of the full hood width. AI will count filters and estimate sizing.

📷 AI Filter Analysis

Stand back so the full hood is visible. Include the hood width in frame. AI will count filter slots and estimate standard sizes.

📷
Tap to take / upload photo Full hood width · straight-on · good lighting

Filter Details (confirm or adjust)

🪣 G.T.O. — Grease Trap Survey
Take a photo of the trap lid with a reference object. AI estimates gallonage.

📷 AI Trap Sizing

Place a tape measure, boot, or hand near the lid for scale. AI will estimate trap size and pump frequency.

📷
Tap to take / upload photo Trap lid · reference object in frame · top-down angle
